title: oxalis imbricata var. imbricata
intro: Oxalis imbricata var. imbricata, also known as the grassleaf wood sorrel or grass-leaved oxalis, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to South Africa and is used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Oxalis imbricata var. imbricata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water to release its medicinal properties.
